channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Australian Open: Aryna Sabalenka overpowers Paula Badosa to near 3rd consecutive title in Melbourne
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Aryna Sabalenka moved one win away from becoming the first woman since 1999 to win three consecutive Australian Open titles, recovering from a slow start to beat good friend Paula Badosa 6-4, 6-2 Thursday night and return to the final. “I have goosebumps.
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Runnin' Utes crushed by #7 Houston, 70-36
item.description: HOUSTON (ABC4 Sports) - Riding a three-game winning streak, the Runnin' Utes had gained some confidence the last couple of weeks. But Wednesday night against #7 Houston, the Utes came crashing back down to Earth. Milos Uzan scored 14 points, L.J. Cryer added 13 points and No.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Heat suspend Jimmy Butler again after missing flight for road trip: report
item.description: The Miami Heat have reportedly docked Jimmy Butler, who was suspended seven games earlier this season, another two games after missing a road trip flight on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Students receive career lessons at Farmers Insurance Open
item.description: About 30 local students got more than a chance to watch golf at Torrey Pines on Wednesday — they got valuable career insights that could shape their futures.
item.dc:creator: Tony Shin, Fox 5

item.title: San Diego native signs with SDFC
item.description: Midfielder Luca de la Torre is coming back to San Diego to play for his hometown Major League Soccer team.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, Fox 5

item.title: Ohtani informed the Los Angeles Dodgers they had landed prized Japanese pitcher Roki Sasaki
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Shohei Ohtani broke the news to the Los Angeles Dodgers that prized Japanese pitcher Roki Sasaki was joining the team. Ohtani sent a text to Dodgers president of baseball operations Andrew Friedman even before Sasaki's agent informed the club.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pro Picks: Sticking with the preseason prediction of an Eagles-Chiefs Super Bowl rematch
item.description: Familiar foes are facing off in the conference championship games in the NFL playoffs. Patrick Mahomes and the two-time defending Super Bowl champion Kansas City Chiefs take on Josh Allen and the Buffalo Bills for the fourth time in the past five postseasons.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ludvig Aberg cards an opening-round 63 for a 2-shot lead at the Farmers Insurance Open
item.description: Ludvig Aberg shot a 9-under 63 for a two-shot lead over Danny Walker and Hayden Springer in the opening round of the Farmers Insurance Open at Torrey Pines.
item.dc:creator: Greg Beacham, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Lions defensive coordinator Aaron Glenn agrees to terms with Jets to be their head coach
item.description: Aaron Glenn is back where his NFL journey began nearly 31 years ago. He was a game-changing cornerback for the New York Jets then. Now he's tasked with helping turn around the fortunes of the franchise.
item.dc:creator: DENNIS WASZAK Jr., Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Man City sent to brink of Champions League exit by PSG. Real Madrid routs Salzburg to advance
item.description: Manchester City is on the brink of a humiliating Champions League exit after a stunning loss to Paris Saint-Germain on Wednesday, and Real Madrid is no longer at risk after routing Salzburg.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM DUNBAR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Raiders hire Bucs assistant general manager John Spytek as their GM, AP source says
item.description: HENDERSON, Nev. (AP) — The Las Vegas Raiders hired Tampa Bay Buccaneers assistant general manager John Spytek to be their GM, a person with knowledge of the situation told The Associated Press on Wednesday. The person spoke on condition of anonymity because the hire has not been announced.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jaguars 'respectfully separate' from general manager Trent Baalke as other teams fill similar roles
item.description: The Jacksonville Jaguars announced Tuesday they have "respectfully separated" with general manager Trent Baalke despite the season ending two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Raiders expected to hire John Spytek as new GM: reports
item.description: Tampa Bay Buccaneers assistant GM John Spytek will get his first opportunity to run a team himself. The Las Vegas Raiders are expected to hire him as their new GM.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tony La Russa’s celebrity concert launches new animal-rescue project after bitter ARF breakup
item.description: This Saturday, Tony La Russa and his family will revive a tradition of fundraising for animal rescue efforts by hosting a star-studded concert that also features a tribute to the late Oakland A’s legend Rickey Henderson.
item.dc:creator: Martha Ross, The Mercury News

item.title: AP's all-College Football Playoff team features an all-Ohio State offensive backfield
item.description: Ohio State’s run to its first national championship in a decade enabled the Buckeyes to dominate the Associated Press’ all-College Football Playoff team.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As Wemby Week continues in Paris, the Spurs star is handling it all with apparent ease
item.description: Here are some of the things on Victor Wembanyama's Paris itinerary to this point: arranging a shopping trip and then a dinner for San Antonio Spurs teammates on Monday, dedicating a pair of outdoor courts in his hometown and then some Fashion Week appearances on Tuesday and heading to the Paris Sain
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former Phillies general manager Ruben Amaro Jr. will launch a hitting competition
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Sluggball, a 4-on-4 baseball competition co-founded by former Philadelphia Phillies general manager Ruben Amaro Jr., is expected to debut in May and tour minor league stadiums this season. Sluggball focuses entirely on situational hitting, with no fielding or running required.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sen Blackburn expects more Democrats to vote for bills protecting women and girls athletes from transgenders
item.description: Sen. Marsha Blackburn expects and hopes more Democrat lawmakers will vote to support bills that protect women and girls in sports from transgenders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
